# Parley

> Multilateral consensus for AI agents with conflicting, private interests. Deterministic red
> lines, max-min consensus over masked verdicts, and a tamper-evident, replayable transcript.
> No cryptocurrency or blockchain — self-hosted, zero-dependency Python core, Apache-2.0.

Parley lets several AI agents, each representing an owner with opposing private interests,
reach a decision that none of them can rig — without any of them revealing their private
limits to the others or to the coordinator. Red lines are code predicates, not LLM discretion,
so a violating option can never win. When no option is feasible for everyone, the result is an
honest deadlock, never a forced bad decision. Every owner can independently verify afterward
that their own limits were never crossed, using only their own private data and the public
transcript.

Not affiliated with, and not to be confused with: any cryptocurrency/blockchain "Parley
Protocol" agent-payments project, "Parley Pro" (legal contract-lifecycle software), or "Parlay
Protocol" (fintech lending). This project has no token, no smart contracts, and no on-chain
component of any kind.

## Try it

- [Live interactive demo](https://parleyprotocol.com/) — run a real decision end to end in the
  browser, watch a cheat option get blocked by a red line, verify the transcript yourself.

## Source and docs

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/5uper0/parley) — full source, Apache-2.0 licensed
- [README](https://github.com/5uper0/parley/blob/main/README.md) — architecture, quickstart,
  the design invariants (hard/soft split, masking, max-min consensus, verifiable transcript)
- [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/5uper0/parley/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)
- [GitHub Discussions](https://github.com/5uper0/parley/discussions) — questions, ideas, contact
